title: FFPE Tissue Sectioning for Staining
author: Marda Jorgensen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8kvhuw6

steps.title: Prepare a water bath at 42°C...
steps.instruction: Prepare a water bath at 42°C and place it next to the Microtome.

steps.title: Insert a new blade for sectioning each block
steps.instruction: Insert a new blade for sectioning each block

steps.title: Section the tissue between 4-5 µm thick
steps.instruction: Section the tissue between 4-5 µm thick.

steps.title: Place the sectioned tissue in the...
steps.instruction: Place the sectioned tissue in the water bath for a few seconds and observe it expanding.

steps.title: When the observed expansion is enough...
steps.instruction: When the observed expansion is enough to remove folds and wrinkles from the tissue, quickly place positive charged microscope slide in the water bath and gently move it upward towards the tissue.

steps.title: Doing so, the tissue will lay...
steps.instruction: Doing so, the tissue will lay on the slide as this is moved out from the water bath.

steps.title: Put the slides upright in a...
steps.instruction: Put the slides upright in a rack to let air dry overnight or dry in 50°C oven for 1 hour.
